tex:
(a) Alex invests \$400 at a rate of 2.3\% per year simple interest.
\newline

Find the total amount Alex has at the end of 5 years.
\newline

(b) Virat has \$100 to spend.
\newline

In February he spends \$$x$ .
\newline

In March he spends 10\% more than he spends in February.
\newline

In April he spends 10\% more than he spends in March.
\newline

At the end of April, Virat has \$33.80 remaining.
\newline

Find the value of $x$.
\newline

(c) Bobbie invests \$500 in an account that pays compound interest each year.
\newline

At the end of 17 years, the value of Bobbie's investment is \$700.13 .
\newline

Find the value of Bobbie's investment at the end of 20 years.
\newline

content.content:
Alex invests \$400 at a rate of 2.3\% per year simple interest.
\newline

Find the total amount Alex has at the end of 5 years.
\newline

Virat has \$100 to spend.
\newline

In February he spends \$$x$ .
\newline

In March he spends 10\% more than he spends in February.
\newline

In April he spends 10\% more than he spends in March.
\newline

At the end of April, Virat has \$33.80 remaining.
\newline

Find the value of $x$.
\newline

Bobbie invests \$500 in an account that pays compound interest each year.
\newline

At the end of 17 years, the value of Bobbie's investment is \$700.13 .
\newline

Find the value of Bobbie's investment at the end of 20 years.
\newline

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}

\item Alex invests \$400 at a rate of 2.3\% per year simple interest.

\vgap

Find the total amount Alex has at the end of 5 years.
\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{3}

\item Virat has \$100 to spend.

\vgap

In February he spends \$$x$ .

In March he spends 10\% more than he spends in February.

In April he spends 10\% more than he spends in March.

\vgap

At the end of April, Virat has \$33.80 remaining.

\vgap

Find the value of $x$.
\AnswerLine[$x = $][]
\Marks{3}

\item Bobbie invests \$500 in an account that pays compound interest each year.

At the end of 17 years, the value of Bobbie's investment is \$700.13 .

\vgap

Find the value of Bobbie's investment at the end of 20 years.
\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{4}

\end{parts}
